title: Garlicky Mushroom Turkish Cheese Flaky Borek
description: A date-night Turkish dish featuring Thai red curry paste, yuzu-cure, and a flaky, buttery crust. Perfect for a low-sodium friendly dinner.
instructions: Roll out the flaky pastry dough to a thickness of 1/8 inch.
Cut the dough into 12 equal rectangles, about 3 inches wide and 5 inches long.
In a small bowl, mix together the softened butter, Thai red curry paste, minced garlic, and a pinch of salt and pepper.
Spread a tablespoon of the butter mixture onto each dough rectangle, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Top each dough rectangle with a slice of mushroom, a sprinkle of yuzu-cured cheese, and a few leaves of parsley.
Fold the dough rectangles in half to form triangles, and press the edges together to seal.
Brush the tops of the borek with a little bit of water, and sprinkle with kosher salt.
Place the borek on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
Serve warm, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
